a_image.alt: Terminal CLI Aesthetic style mood board — Developer Tool · Command Line

sections.a_eyebrow: Monospaced · Dark Mode · Technical · Code Style · Professional
sections.a_description: A clean terminal window with a blinking cursor, soft green output text on a dark canvas, and the satisfying precision of monospaced type — Terminal CLI Aesthetic brings the polished developer experience of modern terminal emulators to web design. This isn't the Hollywood hacker terminal; it's the refined, carefully themed workspace of a developer who takes their dotfiles seriously.

Terminal CLI styling dominates developer tool landing pages, CLI documentation sites, and programming tutorial platforms. You'll find it on code editor marketing pages, DevOps dashboards, open-source project homepages, and any technical product that wants to speak directly to developers in a visual language they already live in every day.

a_image_cover.alt: Terminal CLI Aesthetic style use case illustration
Terminal CLI Aesthetic style use case illustration
Terminal CLI Aesthetic style use case illustration

sections.a_description: A clean, modern terminal emulator — the aesthetic of iTerm2, Warp, or Hyper terminal with a dark background and carefully chosen monospace type. Background: near-black with a blue-gray tint (#1e1e2e) — not pure black, slightly softer. The .inner panel has a faint top border simulating a terminal window title bar: a thin strip with three dots (red, yellow, green — the classic macOS window controls) created via a pseudo-element with colored box-shadows. Text is soft green-white (#a6e3a1) for the primary terminal output color, with white (#cdd6f4) for headings. A blinking cursor effect on the heading via an animated pseudo-element. Clean, minimal — this is a polished developer terminal, not a hacker movie. Set in JetBrains Mono — the definitive modern developer font, designed for code readability with distinctive character shapes and optional ligatures. All text in JetBrains Mono. Heading at 24px, weight 700, line-height 1.3, color #cdd6f4. A ::after pseudo-element adds a blinking block cursor. Eyebrow at 12px, weight 400, color #89b4fa (soft blue), with a $ prefix. Body at 14px, weight 400, line-height 1.7, color #a6e3a1. Buttons: primary — soft blue (#89b4fa) background, dark text, 4px border-radius. Secondary: transparent, subtle border. Alternatives: Fira Code (ligatures), IBM Plex Mono (wider), Source Code Pro (Adobe's take).
